rationale: The citing paper appears to build directly on the cited study’s core setting and approach: both use the same UK retailer context, Feb–Mar 2015 data period, and the five-reviews-per-page crowd-out mechanism for causal identification. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior individual-review effects on purchase to search plus purchase behavior, which aligns closely with the cited paper’s individual-level review-reading and conversion focus (exact DOI match: 10.1177/0022243719866690).
